Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

Software Architecture AI Day [https://softwarearchitecture4ai-day.com/] 2026

Erik Wilde
Head of Enterprise Strategy, Jentic

March 10, 2026

Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(2) Erik Wilde

  • Head of Enterprise Strategy at Jentic [https://jentic.com/]
  • Ambassador for the OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) [https://www.openapis.org/]
  • linkedin.com/in/erikwilde [https://www.linkedin.com/in/erikwilde]
  • youtube.com/ErikWilde [https://www.youtube.com/ErikWilde]


MCP: Start Here

Outline (MCP: Start Here)

  1. MCP: Start Here [4]
  2. Platform Engineering for AI [4]
  3. The Right Tool Access [3]
  4. Workflows: The Right Abstraction [4]
MCP: Start Here Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(4) MCP: LLMs and the Outside World

  • MCP is the standardized way for LLMs to interact with their environment
  • Published about a year ago — rapid traction across tools and platforms
  • Started with modest goals: local filesystem, simple context tools
  • APIs are a key part of the outside world — MCP covers this too


MCP: Start Here Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(5) Mapping APIs to MCP Tools

  • Take your OpenAPI description, expose each operation as an MCP tool
  • Zero development effort — many tools generate MCP servers automatically
  • Agents can now call your APIs through standardized tool use
  • This works — technically


MCP: Start Here Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(6) But Context Runs Out

  • A single complex API already strains the LLM's context window
  • A full organizational API landscape — hundreds of APIs — makes it far worse
  • LLMs cannot effectively reason over thousands of available tools
  • Mechanical endpoint mapping is a starting point — not a strategy


MCP: Start Here Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(7) Is MCP Here to Stay?

  • MCP gained rapid traction — but the tooling landscape is still evolving fast
  • OpenClaw (announced just weeks ago) bypasses MCP entirely
    • Accesses APIs directly — no MCP layer needed
    • A reminder that today's standard may not be tomorrow's
  • We don't know yet whether MCP will be the long-term standard
  • Which raises the question: what should you actually invest in?


Platform Engineering for AI

Outline (Platform Engineering for AI)

  1. MCP: Start Here [4]
  2. Platform Engineering for AI [4]
  3. The Right Tool Access [3]
  4. Workflows: The Right Abstraction [4]
Platform Engineering for AI Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(9) Build for the Platform, Not the Protocol

  • Platform engineering: give teams a great toolbox and let them do great things with it
  • A good platform increases delivery velocity and enables unexpected innovations
  • AI enablement should be part of the platform — not bolted on as a protocol-specific layer
  • Whether agents use MCP, OpenClaw, or something we have not seen yet — the platform serves them all
  • Consumption-agnostic: build once, serve many


Platform Engineering for AI Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(10) You Still Need an Isolation Layer

  • Whether via MCP or direct access, agents need a controlled entry point into your APIs
  • Direct API access means credentials live inside the agent — that is not where they belong
  • An isolation layer keeps access control centralized and auditable
  • This is consumption-agnostic: the layer works the same regardless of how the agent connects


Platform Engineering for AI Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(11) APIs Have Inconsistent Security — Your Layer Can Fix That

  • Some APIs have fine-grained OAuth scopes; others have a single token that does everything
  • As a consumer of APIs you don't own, you cannot change their built-in security model
  • But your isolation layer can impose a custom security model on top
    • This agent can only access these three endpoints
    • Fine-grained, per-agent access control — regardless of what the underlying API provides


Platform Engineering for AI Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(12) Isolation Does Not Solve Scale

  • Even with proper isolation and a clean access layer…
  • A large API landscape still overwhelms an LLM choosing from thousands of tools
  • The problem has shifted: from access to discovery
  • We need smarter ways to surface the right tools at the right time


The Right Tool Access

Outline (The Right Tool Access)

  1. MCP: Start Here [4]
  2. Platform Engineering for AI [4]
  3. The Right Tool Access [3]
  4. Workflows: The Right Abstraction [4]
The Right Tool Access Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(14) Just-in-Time Discovery: The Tool Tool

  • Instead of exposing all tools upfront, expose a single search capability
  • The agent declares what it wants to accomplish
  • Search returns a small, targeted set of tools for that specific task
  • Solves the context explosion problem at any landscape scale
  • Anthropic calls this advanced tool use; FastMCP recently announced support
  • At Jentic [https://jentic.com/], we have been building on this pattern for about a year


The Right Tool Access Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(15) Make More Available

  • Just-in-time discovery only helps if the tools are actually there to find
  • Make your full API landscape available and searchable through the platform
  • Surface your capabilities — even before optimizing them
  • More available means more value agents can extract


The Right Tool Access Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(16) Describe Tools for Agents, Not Humans

  • Descriptions written for human developers do not work well for LLM agents
  • Agents need descriptions that explain purpose, context, and when to use a tool
  • This does not require changing your APIs — just how you describe them
  • The API Scorecard: a structured way to evaluate and improve API descriptions for AI


Workflows: The Right Abstraction

Outline (Workflows: The Right Abstraction)

  1. MCP: Start Here [4]
  2. Platform Engineering for AI [4]
  3. The Right Tool Access [3]
  4. Workflows: The Right Abstraction [4]
Workflows: The Right Abstraction Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(18) The Workflow Gap

  • Even with just-in-time discovery and good descriptions…
  • Low-level APIs often require 10–20 chained calls to accomplish a single task
  • The agent must figure out the sequence, handle errors, and manage state
  • This is the next frontier of the AI enablement challenge


Workflows: The Right Abstraction Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(19) GitHub: 1,300 Endpoints → 60 Tools

  • The GitHub REST API has around 1,300 endpoints and 1,700 operations
  • The GitHub MCP server exposes around 60 tools
  • Good MCP servers already think in workflows, not endpoints
  • Each tool represents something a developer wants to do — not a raw API call
  • This is "jobs to be done" thinking applied to AI tool design


Workflows: The Right Abstraction Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(20) Push Workflows to the API Level

  • MCP workflows are LLM-consumer-only: agents talk to the MCP server, developers do not
  • Instead: implement workflows at the API and platform level
    • Same design effort — much broader reuse
    • Regular software, agents, and MCP consumers all benefit from the same workflow
  • Do not invest in making things work for LLMs — invest in making things work for everyone


Workflows: The Right Abstraction Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(21) This Is Platform Engineering for AI

  • Build it once into the platform
  • Agents get high-level, workflow-oriented capabilities
  • Developers get reusable, well-defined building blocks
  • Works regardless of whether the consumer uses MCP, OpenClaw, or something not yet invented
  • Start this journey now — the sooner you invest, the better positioned you will be


Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(22) Thank You!
